ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a shiitake mushroom food product class but no specific grilled/skewered preparation subclass; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers shiitake mushroom as a commodity; no grilled preparation facet applied; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT code 412061001 covers shiitake mushroom as a substance; no grilled preparation concept available; closest_parent used.

serving.description: 3 skewers (approximately 3 medium shiitake mushrooms per skewer)
serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is 3 skewers of grilled shiitake mushroom, each skewer holding approximately one medium mushroom cap, totalling roughly 90 g.

nutrient_source.title: Mushrooms, shiitake, cooked, without salt — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 169403)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for cooked shiitake mushroom (FDC ID 169403) used as the closest approved match for grilled shiitake mushroom. Grilling without added fat or salt produces a nutritional profile closely comparable to boiled/cooked shiitake; minor moisture loss during grilling may slightly concentrate nutrients but values are treated as representative. No added oil or seasoning assumed based on the menu description 'Three String / 3串'.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for grilled shiitake mushroom was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. Shiitake mushroom (cooked) has a reported GI of approximately 28 (low) based on Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables, which include mushroom entries. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (28 × 12.95) / 100 ≈ 3.6, where carbohydrate per 90 g serving = 14.39 × 0.9 = 12.95 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (9 mg per 100 g) and high potassium (304 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Grilled shiitake mushroom is an excellent choice for individuals with hypertension due to its very low sodium content and favourable potassium-to-sodium ratio, which supports healthy blood pressure regulation; patients should ensure no high-sodium sauces or marinades are added during preparation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (GI 28) and moderate dietary fibre (2.2 g per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a low GI of 28 and a glycemic load of approximately 3.6 per 3-skewer serving, grilled shiitake mushroom produces a minimal postprandial glucose response and is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes as part of a balanced meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat (0.05 g per 100 g), zero cholesterol, and presence of beta-glucan dietary fibre
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Grilled shiitake mushroom is cardioprotective due to its negligible saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and beta-glucan content, which has been associated with LDL cholesterol reduction; it is a heart-healthy food suitable for individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grilled Shiitake Mushroom (烤香菇) is a simple, minimally processed Chinese dish consisting of fresh shiitake mushrooms threaded onto skewers and cooked over a grill or open flame. In the Singapore hawker and restaurant context, it is commonly sold as a set of three skewers (3串), each holding one medium shiitake mushroom cap. The grilling process imparts a smoky, umami-rich flavour and produces a tender yet slightly chewy texture characteristic of cooked shiitake. Shiitake mushrooms (Lentinula edodes) are prized in Chinese cuisine for their deep savoury taste, derived from naturally occurring glutamates and lentinan. Nutritionally, grilled shiitake mushroom is low in calories (approximately 56 kcal per 100 g), very low in fat (0.22 g per 100 g), and provides a moderate amount of carbohydrate (14.39 g per 100 g) with a low glycemic index of approximately 28, making it suitable for blood glucose management. It is a good source of B vitamins, particularly niacin (3.88 mg per 100 g) and riboflavin (0.21 mg per 100 g), as well as potassium (304 mg per 100 g), zinc (1.33 mg per 100 g), and selenium (5.7 mcg per 100 g). The dish is naturally vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Its very low sodium content (9 mg per 100 g) makes it appropriate for individuals managing hypertension. The beta-glucan fibre content of shiitake has been associated with immune modulation and LDL cholesterol reduction, supporting cardiovascular health. This dish is widely available at Chinese restaurants and hawker stalls in Singapore, including at establishments such as Wan Jia Fu Chinese Restaurant.
common_user_queries: What are the calories in grilled mushroom?
Is grilled mushroom suitable for diabetics?
What is the GI of mushroom?
How much protein is in grilled shiitake?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cooked shiitake mushroom (FDC ID 169403), which is the closest approved source match. No Singapore-specific HPB Foodo entry for grilled shiitake mushroom was found. GI is an analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Confidence is medium rather than high because no exact grilled preparation entry was found in a primary regional source.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are based on USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 169403 (cooked shiitake mushroom without salt) as the closest approved match; grilling without added fat or salt is assumed per the menu description. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central source; mushrooms contain no trans fats by nature (naturally zero). 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as mushrooms are a plant-derived food with no dietary cholesterol. 4. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; shiitake mushrooms contain no preformed vitamin A (naturally zero). 5. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero as shiitake mushrooms are a plant-derived food and contain no vitamin B12 by nature. 6. monounsaturated_fat_g (0.03), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.10), potassium_mg (304.0), calcium_mg (3.0), iron_mg (0.44), vitamin_c_mg (0.3), vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg (0.02), vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg (0.21), vitamin_b3_niacin_mg (3.88), vitamin_b6_mg (0.24), folate_mcg_dfe (14.0), vitamin_d_mcg (0.4), vitamin_e_mg (0.10), phosphorus_mg (112.0), magnesium_mg (20.0), zinc_mg (1.33), selenium_mcg (5.7), water_g (83.5) are all reported from USDA FDC ID 169403. 7. GI of 28 is an analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables; no direct University of Sydney GI Database entry for grilled shiitake was found. 8. Serving size of 90 g is estimated based on 3 skewers with one medium shiitake cap per skewer (~30 g each). 9. The input record listed cuisine_tags including 'Roasted Duck', which appears to be a restaurant-level tag from field survey and is not relevant to this specific dish; it has been disregarded. 10. No -1 sentinel values are used for fields where USDA FDC ID 169403 provides data; -1 is not present in this record.
